stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 42% and 61%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $246K in good team experience and project size, $42K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $80K in good team experience and project size, $25K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $199K in good team experience and project size, $68K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.52: best option is Agile with EV=$148.1K. Agile is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [127.7, 166.4].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Agile is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
